title: heracleum lanatum subsp. moellendorffii
intro: Heracleum lanatum subsp. moellendorffii, also known as Korean angelica or Korean angelica root, is a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae). This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Heracleum lanatum subsp. moellendorffii include its ability to alleviate symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Heracleum lanatum subsp. moellendorffii is typically prepared as a powder or extract for use in herbal remedies, and it can also be consumed as a tea or added to soups and stews.
